Documentation_Quality: Poor (empty README; Readme.txt with product specs)
Source_Description: Promotional subset of Appen TUR_ASR003_CN commercial product; read Turkish news prompts recorded on mobile phones
Copyright_Concerns: Yes (commercial product; no redistribution rights documented)
Notes: Severely mislabeled: title says 700h but only 5.6h uploaded (0.8% of full product). Clean audio quality but unknown license from commercial vendor.

Source_Description: YouTube-scraped from @BirDinle channel; narration of Otlakci by Memduh Sevket Esendal (public domain text, 1946)
Copyright_Concerns: Yes (audio recordings are copyrighted by YouTube channel owner; no permission documented)
Notes: Text is public domain (author died 1952) but audio performances are separate copyrighted works. Same uploader's Synthetic dataset has CC-BY-4.0 but this one deliberately omits license.

Source_Description: 1K-sample subset of Common Voice 17 Turkish; pre-tokenized with SNAC codec for Orpheus TTS fine-tuning
Notes: No raw audio; only SNAC+Llama token sequences. Usable exclusively with Orpheus TTS model family. Too small for standalone training.

Source_Description: Same 1K-sample subset as D03a but with raw audio preserved; audio durations 1.33-10.2s
Notes: Raw audio+text pairs but only 1K samples. Marginal utility as evaluation set.

Source_Description: Audio extracted from Bentropi YouTube documentary channel (~555K subscribers). Topics: historical mysteries, true crime, cultural documentaries.
Copyright_Concerns: Yes (monetized YouTube channel; no permission documented; YouTube ToS violation)
Notes: Split across 11 separate HuggingFace repos. Sub-second segmentation artifacts (0.04s clips). Column inconsistencies across parts. Combined ~578MB.

Source_Description: Audio from Tunc Kurt Matematik YouTube channel (~1.14M subscribers). Math education content (TYT, AYT, calculus).
Notes: Largest single falan42 dataset. Used to fine-tune SpeechT5 model (MIT license on model, not data). Narrow domain (mathematics only).

Source_Description: Educational YouTube livestream speech. Informal teacher-to-student dialogue. Also duplicated by oati41 (byte-for-byte identical).
Notes: Duration range 0.08-60s indicates both segmentation errors and unsegmented clips. Too small for TTS training.

Source_Description: Aggregation of 7 sources: 3 MIT single-speaker (Mazlum Kiper, Ahmet Deniz, Nisan Kumru), Khan Academy Turkish (NC-SA), Common Voice 17 (CC0), 2 tiny Derya sets (no license)
Copyright_Concerns: Partial (Khan Academy NC restriction not reflected in declared license; 2 sources have no license)
Notes: Most convenient Turkish TTS aggregation on HuggingFace. License is mislabeled: Khan Academy forces NonCommercial. 16kHz sources upsampled to 24kHz. No speaker IDs. ISSAI corpus (218h, MIT) is larger and cleaner-licensed.

Source_Description: SNAC-tokenized version of D07 for Orpheus-3B fine-tuning. 7 tokens per audio frame, 4096 codebook.
Notes: 1:1 correspondence with D07. License correctly adds NonCommercial. Usable only with Orpheus-family models.

Speakers: 4 synthetic (ali M, zeynep F, leyla F, alev F)
Documentation_Quality: Moderate (speaker stats and domains listed; TTS system undisclosed)
Source_Description: LLM-generated text across 13 business domains (finance, customer service, ecommerce, telecom, etc.) synthesized by undisclosed TTS system
Notes: Cleanest license of any Turkish TTS dataset. However: TTS system undisclosed, synthetic-on-synthetic risk if used to train another TTS, domain bias toward customer service language.

Source_Description: Pre-computed mel-spectrograms + 512-dim x-vector speaker embeddings. Preprocessed specifically for Microsoft SpeechT5 fine-tuning following HuggingFace official tutorial.
Notes: No raw audio recoverable. Only value: confirms SpeechT5 was fine-tuned on Turkish. One of few datasets with proper train/test split.
